followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I keep the same broad verdict but slightly lower the score because the other review sharpened two real issues: the q/w definitions needed for reproducibility, and the fact that the temperature variation of alpha is not merely speculative but directly undermines the interpretation of alpha as a universal GUP parameter.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: The claim that the Debye model fails at very low temperature is too broad; the Debye model is often most controlled in the low-temperature regime, though real-material corrections still matter.

The suggestion that GUP-modified QED corrections to Bethe-Bloch are a central missing ingredient is speculative from the evidence in the paper; air-shower and muography-systematic issues are better-grounded objections.

Calling the Ref. [21] bulk modulus simply an ab initio value is not established by the bundle, since the paper describes it as an experimental reference value reported in that source. The real objection is dataset compatibility and calibration, not that it is definitely theoretical.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The undefined polylogarithm argument w and missing numerical/source value for q are concrete reproducibility gaps in the modified Debye calculation.
The nearly two-order-of-magnitude temperature dependence of the inferred alpha should be treated as a major interpretive problem if alpha is claimed as a fundamental GUP parameter.
The novelty is incremental over earlier GUP thermodynamics and seismic-wave constraint work; the contribution is mainly the muography-density coupling.
The density error budget is incomplete, especially given the limited detector channel count in Ref. [34] and missing seismic velocity uncertainties.

main_claims.claim: Quadratic GUP/deformed phase-space measure modifies Debye vibrational free energy and thus the isothermal bulk modulus of a crystal.
main_claims.notes: The broad chain is plausible and consistent with GUP thermodynamics literature, but the derivation is too compressed. The volume-dependence assumptions, definition of w in the polylogarithms, numerical value/source of q for aluminum, and order in alpha are not sufficiently explicit for audit.
main_claims.paper_location: Equations (3)-(8), especially the modified zero-point energy, F_vib^mod, P, and K formulae

main_claims.claim: Muon tomography plus P- and S-wave velocity measurements remove the density degeneracy in extracting elastic moduli and give gravity-independent thermodynamic inputs.
main_claims.notes: The algebraic role of density is standard, and muography is plausibly less directly gravitational than weighing. The stronger claim of gravity independence is overstated because muon flux modeling, detector acceptance, path length, composition, calibration, and possible LIV/GUP-related air-shower effects are not folded into the error budget.
main_claims.paper_location: Equations before and at Eq. (9), and paragraphs beginning 'Most standard methods...' and 'One of such a strategy could be potentially muography'

main_claims.claim: Existing aluminum data from Ref. [34], plus a reference bulk modulus, give constraints on the gravity/GUP parameter comparable to current laboratory experiments.
main_claims.notes: The numerical inversion is not reproducible from the presented text. Several needed values are missing or poorly rendered; no input table, q value, explicit inversion formula, uncertainty propagation, or parameter-convention comparison to Ref. [16] is provided.
main_claims.paper_location: Paragraphs beginning 'In [34]...' and 'It is straightforward to determine the value of the parameter from \eqref kbulk', and Fig. 1

main_claims.claim: The inferred temperature dependence of alpha may reflect physical quantum-gravity sensitivity, phonon effective mass, or phase-space deformation effects.
main_claims.notes: The paper acknowledges a nearly two-order-of-magnitude change and then offers qualitative explanations. If alpha is meant as a universal GUP parameter, this is a serious interpretive problem; if it is an effective material/temperature parameter, the claimed bound on fundamental gravity is weakened.
main_claims.paper_location: Fig. 1 and the paragraphs following it

main_claims.claim: The Debye temperature can be obtained from the same seismic/muon dataset rather than from specific heat or X-ray Debye-Waller methods that may themselves contain gravity-model dependence.
main_claims.notes: The formula using mean sound velocity is standard in spirit, but the claim inherits the same systematics as the velocity and density measurements and still assumes the Debye approximation is adequate for aluminum under the chosen conditions.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Alpha is a universal, temperature-independent GUP parameter rather than a material-, quasiparticle-, or temperature-dependent effective parameter.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Equations (3)-(8), numerical extraction paragraphs, and Fig. 1 discussion
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The paper reports different inferred values at different temperatures; without this assumption and a consistency test, the result is not a clean bound on fundamental quantum gravity.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The Debye/anharmonic model accurately describes real aluminum over the temperatures and sample conditions used in the proposed inference.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Ordinary thermal expansion, defects, impurities, elastic anisotropy, anharmonicity, and Debye-model error could absorb or mimic the alpha correction.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The anharmonicity parameter q and Gruneisen parameter gamma used for aluminum are known under the same experimental conditions as the muon/seismic measurements.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Bulk-modulus equation and the definition of q
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: q appears directly in the K(alpha) expression; an unstated or externally borrowed q value can materially alter the inferred alpha.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Muon-inferred density and seismic velocities have a complete enough uncertainty budget for the alpha constraint.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Ref. [34] discussion and footnote noting missing velocity errors
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If detector, geometry, flux, composition, or velocity uncertainties dominate, the quoted sensitivity is overstated.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The reference bulk modulus used for comparison is compatible with the proposed gravity-independent measurement philosophy and the same thermodynamic state.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Paragraph adopting the reference value from Ref. [21]
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: A mismatched external K can reintroduce calibration dependence and make the inferred residual look like a GUP correction.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do the PDF or TeX source define w, list the numerical value/source for q, and provide complete numerical values that were lost in the arXiv HTML rendering?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The supplied HTML bundle has visible equation and inline-variable loss; a human should inspect the PDF/source before attributing all omissions to the paper itself.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does differentiating the stated GUP-modified Helmholtz free energy actually yield the displayed bulk-modulus formula, including signs, constants, gamma/q terms, and alpha order?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires line-by-line symbolic verification of the thermodynamic derivation beyond what is explicit in the 4-page text.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are the quoted alpha bounds comparable to existing laboratory GUP bounds under the same parameter convention and units?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper references Ref. [16] but does not provide a conversion table or normalization details; comparison requires checking the cited conventions.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Can ordinary temperature dependence of aluminum elastic constants and anharmonic terms account for the trend in Fig. 1 without invoking quantum-gravity physics?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This needs a materials-physics calculation or data comparison not present in the bundle.

novelty_assessment: The most novel element is the proposal to use muography as the density input in a seismic/elastic GUP test. That is a real and clever combination. The underlying GUP-modified Debye thermodynamics and seismic-constraint strategy appear largely inherited from prior work, and the data come from an existing laboratory study. I therefore see the novelty as genuine but incremental: worthwhile as a proposal, not yet a strong new constraint result.
score_rationale: The idea is imaginative and potentially useful, but the current letter is too thin to support the advertised constraint. The main downgrade from cycle 1 is that the temperature dependence of the inferred alpha now looks like a central unresolved tension, not just a loose interpretation issue. Reproducibility also remains poor because key symbols, inputs, and uncertainty propagation are absent or inaccessible. I do not rate it lower because some formula problems may be HTML artifacts and the proposal itself is scientifically interesting.
summary: The paper proposes a short phenomenology program: combine muon-derived density with seismic/ultrasonic wave velocities to obtain elastic inputs for an aluminum Debye-solid calculation, then infer a quadratic-GUP parameter from the modified bulk modulus. After reading the other review, my view is slightly more skeptical about the interpretation of the temperature dependence and numerical reproducibility, but not about the creativity of the proposal. The work is best read as a proof-of-concept idea rather than a robust new laboratory bound.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper itself says the difference is nearly two orders of magnitude and gives only qualitative mechanisms such as phonon effective mass, phase-space-cell deformation, modified dispersion relations, and LIV. No quantitative model separates ordinary material temperature dependence from a GUP signal.
technical_objections.objection: The inferred GUP parameter appears temperature-dependent by nearly two orders of magnitude, which is not reconciled with treating alpha as a fundamental universal parameter.
technical_objections.paper_location: Fig. 1 and the paragraphs beginning 'The sensitivity of the gravity parameter...' and 'One possible explanation...'

technical_objections.evidence: The paper does not provide a full input table, an explicit inversion formula, the numerical q value, a defined polylogarithm argument w, complete uncertainty propagation, or a normalization bridge to existing GUP bounds. The source text also garbles some reported numerical values and units.
technical_objections.objection: The numerical constraint on alpha is not reproducible from the presented information.
technical_objections.paper_location: Paragraph beginning 'It is straightforward to determine the value of the parameter from \eqref kbulk', Fig. 1, and equations (7)-(11)

technical_objections.evidence: K=-V(dP/dV)_T depends on theta(V), gamma(V), q(V), p, alpha, and the logarithmic/polylogarithmic modified terms. The text defines gamma and gestures at q, but does not clearly say which quantities are held fixed or how the modified terms are differentiated.
technical_objections.objection: The derivation of the bulk modulus does not state enough volume-dependence assumptions to check the differentiation.
technical_objections.paper_location: Pressure and bulk-modulus formulae around equations (7)-(8)

technical_objections.evidence: Muon tomography depends on flux models, detector geometry, attenuation/path-length modeling, energy thresholds, composition, calibration, and detector resolution. The paper also notes only three detection channels in Ref. [34] and missing seismic-velocity uncertainties, yet the bound uncertainty is described as coming only from density uncertainty.
technical_objections.objection: The muography-based density is presented as gravity-independent, but the systematics relevant to the proposed bound are not quantified.
technical_objections.paper_location: Paragraphs after Eq. (9), the discussion of Ref. [34], and the LIV paragraph near the end

technical_objections.evidence: For an isotropic elastic medium the standard relations are v_P^2=(K+4S/3)/rho and v_S^2=S/rho. This may be an HTML conversion artifact, so it should not be treated as a certified paper error without PDF inspection.
technical_objections.objection: The elastic-wave equations as rendered are dimensionally inconsistent unless squares or square roots are missing.
technical_objections.paper_location: Equations immediately before Eq. (9), shown as v_P=K+4S/3 rho and v_S=S rho in the HTML bundle

technical_objections.evidence: The comparison K_theory(alpha) versus K_experiment appears to rely on a value not directly obtained from the proposed protocol. The paper does not explain whether that reference value satisfies the same gravity-independence criteria or how dataset mismatch enters the uncertainty budget.
technical_objections.objection: Using an external ambient-temperature/reference bulk modulus while arguing for a muon-plus-seismic gravity-independent protocol introduces an underexplained calibration step.
technical_objections.paper_location: Paragraph beginning 'In [34], the bulk modulus ... However, since the experiment was likely performed at ambient temperature K, we instead adopt the experimental reference value GPa reported in [21]'
